title: podman images: implement --all
body: It looks like `podman images` lists all images by default, including intermediates. docker, OTOH, only lists intermediates when given `--all`. Neither approach is right or wrong, this is simply a design decision. I am filing this issue to ensure that the decision is an active, deliberate one.

comments.body: I think we should follow what Docker does.
we will ... but cannot until buildah produces cached images

It's not in the manpage, so we're consistent at least.

Though maybe we should add a "-a" private option (not visible in the manpage or help text) that does nothing, to make sure people who habitually types `images -a` aren't broken?
Yes lets add the flag. And change our defaults to match docker.
@rhatdan my thinking was to add this when buildah supports cached images and we actually can differentiate between --all and not all . is that cool?
